title: alyssum simplex subsp. edentulum
intro: Alyssum simplex subsp. edentulum, also known as toothless alyssum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antibacterial agent. The health benefits of alyssum simplex subsp. edentulum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve nausea. Alyssum simplex subsp. edentulum can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible addition to a variety of natural health remedies.
